Package com.wip.controller

Class CustomerController

java.lang.Object
com.wip.controller.CustomerController
@RestController @RequestMapping("/api/customers") public class CustomerController extends Object
REST controller for managing customers in the Courier Tracking System.

Exposes RESTful endpoints under /api/customers for full CRUD operations on customer resources. All responses are returned as JSON. Validation is enforced on incoming request bodies via Bean Validation (@Valid). This controller integrates with the OpenAPI (Swagger) documentation via SpringDoc annotations.

Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Dharshan K S (dharshan.ks@wipro.com)

  • Constructor Details

    • CustomerController

      public CustomerController(CustomerService customerService)
      Constructs a CustomerController with the required CustomerService.
      Parameters:
      customerService - the service bean responsible for customer business logic; injected automatically by Spring's dependency injection container

  • Method Details

    • addCustomer

      @PostMapping("/addCust") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<CustomerDto> addCustomer(@Valid @RequestBody @Valid CustomerDto customerDto)
      Creates a new customer record in the system.

      Accepts a validated CustomerDto request body containing customer details such as name, email, phone number, and address. On success, the persisted customer data (including the generated ID) is returned wrapped in a 200 OK response.

      Parameters:
      customerDto - the data transfer object carrying customer details to be persisted; must pass all Bean Validation constraints
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the saved CustomerDto with HTTP 200

    • getAllCustomers

      @GetMapping("/getAll") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<List<CustomerDto>> getAllCustomers()
      Retrieves a list of all customers registered in the system.

      Returns every customer record available in the database, mapped to their respective CustomerDto representations. Returns an empty list when no customers exist.

      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing a List of CustomerDto objects representing all customers, with HTTP 200

    • getCustomerById

      @GetMapping("/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<CustomerDto> getCustomerById(@PathVariable Long id)
      Retrieves the details of a single customer identified by their unique ID.

      Looks up the customer with the given id in the database. If no customer is found with that ID, the service layer throws a ResourceNotFoundException, which results in a 404 Not Found response.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the customer to retrieve; must be a positive Long
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the matching CustomerDto with HTTP 200
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer exists with the given ID (HTTP 404)

    • updateCustomer

      @PutMapping("/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<CustomerDto> updateCustomer(@PathVariable Long id, @Valid @RequestBody @Valid CustomerDto customerDto)
      Updates an existing customer record with new data.

      Applies all non-null fields from the supplied CustomerDto to the customer identified by id. The customer must already exist; otherwise a ResourceNotFoundException is thrown. Bean Validation is enforced on the request body before processing.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the customer to update
      customerDto - the data transfer object containing the updated customer details; must pass all Bean Validation constraints
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity containing the updated CustomerDto with HTTP 200
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer exists with the given ID (HTTP 404)

    • deleteCustomer

      @DeleteMapping("/{id}") public org.springframework.http.ResponseEntity<Void> deleteCustomer(@PathVariable Long id)
      Permanently deletes the customer identified by the given ID.

      Removes the customer record from the system. If the customer has associated parcel records, the service layer may prevent deletion and throw an appropriate exception. On successful deletion, a 204 No Content response is returned with no body.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the customer to delete; must be a positive Long
      Returns:
      a ResponseEntity with no body and HTTP 204 on success
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer exists with the given ID (HTTP 404)
